assertions.text: Both V1 and V2 were traveling northbound on a four-lane, physically divided, bidirectional roadway (two lanes northbound, two lanes southbound).
Both vehicles were negotiating a curve to the right with a slight incline in the roadway at the time of the incident.
V1 was traveling in the leftmost northbound lane (lane one) and V2 was traveling in the adjacent right northbound lane (lane two).
V1 began to move from lane one into lane two while V2 was attempting to pass V1.
V2 departed its lane to the right immediately before the collision.
V2's frontal plane impacted V1's rear plane (V2 struck the rear of V1 from behind).
There was a single impact between V2 and V1 (one distinct collision event described).
After the impact V2 came to final rest off the right side of the roadway.
There was a small fire in the engine compartment of V2 following the collision.
V1 continued northbound after the collision and was not located (V1 departed the scene).
The collision severity included a post-impact small engine-compartment fire in V2 (indicating non-negligible damage to V2).

verdicts.evidence: The sensors provide vehicle speed and EDR metadata but do not record road layout or heading (no GPS heading/lane data). The only quantitative items are vehicle speed ["first_value": 121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 and "last_value": 111.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2) and vehicle metadata showing speed_limit_kmh ["113.0"](T1), but lane configuration and both vehicles' headings are not present, so this assertion is unverifiable.

There is steering input data with a peak of ["35.0 deg" at t=0.0](T2) which shows steering activity, but road curvature and grade (incline) are not recorded by these channels, so negotiation of a curve to the right and a slight incline cannot be verified from the available telemetry.

Lane positions (leftmost lane vs adjacent lane) are not captured in the vehicle telemetry. The speed samples [first 121.0 km/h, last 111.0 km/h](T2) do not provide lane assignment, so this assertion is unverifiable.

The telemetry shows a brake transition at [t = -0.5 s](T2) and steering peak at [30.0 deg at t=0.0](T2), but there is no lateral position or multi-vehicle relative motion data to confirm that V1 moved lanes while V2 attempted to pass; therefore this sequence is unverifiable.

The crash-pulse includes a lateral cumulative delta-V of ["11.0 km/h" at t=0.09 s](T2), indicating some lateral velocity change, but without lane-boundary or GPS lateral position we cannot determine whether V2 departed its lane to the right immediately before collision, so this is unverifiable.

The EDR summary labels the event as ["Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"](T1) and the crash-pulse shows a large longitudinal delta-V with a signed maximum of ["-81.0 km/h" at t=0.16 s](T2) which is consistent with a rear-impact where the subject vehicle experienced a strong longitudinal change; combined these telemetry indicators support that V2's front impacted another vehicle's rear.

The crash data shows a single dominant crash-pulse with a maximum cumulative longitudinal delta-V magnitude of ["81.0 km/h" at t=0.16 s](T2) and the detector returned one impact delta-V event for long axis (plus one for lateral) in the event list (delta-V detections count 2 channels but representing the same collision) (T3); there is no evidence of multiple distinct collision pulses in the window, supporting a single impact.

Final rest position off the right side of the roadway is not recorded by on-vehicle sensors here; while the vehicle speed changed from [121.0 km/h to 111.0 km/h between t=-5.0 and t=0.0](T2) and crash delta-V is recorded, there is no GPS final position so this assertion is unverifiable.

Fire presence is not measured by the vehicle telemetry channels available. The EDR shows airbag deployment [stage1 at 5.0 ms] and belt code [1] (T1) and damage via delta-V [81.0 km/h](T2), but no sensor reports engine fire, so this assertion is unverifiable.

There is no multi-vehicle telemetry from V1 nor scene camera or GPS tracking to show V1 movement after collision; the available V2 data contains no information about V1 continuing northbound or being unlocated, so this is unverifiable.

Telemetry records a substantial crash pulse with maximum cumulative longitudinal delta-V of ["81.0 km/h" at t=0.16 s](T2) and the EDR notes a post-impact airbag event [driver_airbag_stage1_ms = 5.0](T1); while fire itself is unverifiable, the presence of a significant delta-V [81.0 km/h](T2) supports that damage was non-negligible which is consistent with consequential engine-compartment damage.

result.rationale: Sensor data from V2 include clear EDR crash-pulse measurements showing a dominant longitudinal delta-V of ["81.0 km/h" at t=0.16 s](T2) and an event classification ["Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"](T1), supporting impact direction and single-impact conclusions. Most assertions about road geometry, lanes, vehicle positions, fire, and V1 post-crash behavior are unverifiable because GPS/lane-level, multi-vehicle relative data, and fire sensors are not present in the provided telemetry.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
